pi_items.source: Code analysis of seed_bahmni.py: Robert Anderson is the 15th entry in PATIENT_DATA list (index 14). With enumerate(PATIENT_DATA, 1), when processing Robert Anderson, i=15, so identifier = f'BAH{i+1:06d}' = BAH000016. Verified by execution of seeding logic.
seed_bahmni.py line 46: PATIENT_DATA list contains ('Robert', 'Anderson', 'M', 1955) as the 15th patient. Confirmed as patient name in seeding algorithm.

Multiple source files confirm: setup_task.sh line 58 checks for 82kg weight observations, export_result.sh line 40 uses target_value = 82.0, verifier.py line 87 checks 'if abs(val - 82.0) < 0.1'. All task scripts consistently use 82 kg as the target weight.

Multiple source files confirm: setup_task.sh line 14 calculates 'date -d "10 days ago"', export_result.sh line 12 uses same logic, verifier.py line 68 uses 'datetime.now() - timedelta(days=10)'. All task components use 10-day retrospective window.

seed_bahmni.py line 57 defines CONCEPT_UUIDS with 'weight_kg': '5089AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA'. This is the standard CIEL (Comprehensive ICD Enumerated List) concept UUID for weight in kg, documented in OpenMRS wiki and O3 documentation at https://o3-docs.openmrs.org. Web search confirms this UUID is standard in OpenMRS implementations.

privileged_info_summary: The task targets a synthetic patient named Robert Anderson with the correct CIEL concept UUID for weight (5089AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA). Patient names derive from real US Census Bureau public domain data, but all patient records and identifiers are synthetically generated for this task environment. The task correctly expects a retrospective weight measurement of 82 kg recorded 10 days in the past. CRITICAL ERROR: The task metadata specifies patient identifier BAH000015, but the actual seeding algorithm assigns Robert Anderson the identifier BAH000016 based on their position as the 15th patient in the PATIENT_DATA list.
